critic_raw:
The split strategy uses stratified random sampling across 36 months while noting that 'readmission rates vary across seasons'—then proceeding with random sampling that mixes these seasonal periods across splits. The justification ('balances representation from different time periods in each set') describes temporal stratification in name only; the actual split is random, not temporal. For a 36-month dataset, this mixes future and past admissions in training, allowing the model to learn from later clinical periods and be evaluated on earlier ones.

Preprocessing is correctly stated as fit on training data only for imputation, scaling, and aggregation—this is one of the few designs in this set that appears to handle this correctly.

The RNN model receives raw time-series vital signs (4-hour intervals over up to 7 days = 42 timesteps), while the logistic regression baseline receives aggregated statistics from the same data (mean, std, min, max, trend slope). This is not an architecture comparison—it is a feature representation comparison. The RNN may outperform logistic regression simply because it has access to finer-grained temporal resolution, not because recurrent architectures are better. The hypothesis claims RNNs outperform logistic regression, but the design cannot separate the contribution of temporal granularity from the contribution of the recurrent architecture.

Macro-averaged F1 is described as 'threshold-independent,' which is incorrect. F1 score is computed at a specific decision threshold (typically 0.5), not across all thresholds. The design does not specify whether F1 is computed at a fixed threshold, at the validation-optimized threshold, or as a micro/macro average. This ambiguity makes the reported metric non-reproducible.
all_issues_raised: Stratified random sampling across 36 months places later admissions in training and earlier ones in test, violating the causal temporal order required for meaningful deployment-relevant evaluation.

The feature set given to the RNN (raw 4-hour vital sign sequences) versus the logistic regression baseline (aggregated statistics) differs in temporal resolution, not just model architecture; any observed advantage may reflect information granularity rather than recurrent architecture superiority.
Macro-averaged F1 is incorrectly described as 'threshold-independent'; F1 is a threshold-dependent metric, and the design does not specify which threshold is used, making results non-reproducible.
all_issues_adjudicated: Stratified random sampling across 36 months places later admissions in training and earlier ones in test, violating the causal temporal order required for meaningful deployment-relevant evaluation.

The feature set given to the RNN (raw 4-hour vital sign sequences) versus the logistic regression baseline (aggregated statistics) differs in temporal resolution, not just model architecture; any observed advantage may reflect information granularity rather than recurrent architecture superiority.
Macro-averaged F1 is incorrectly described as 'threshold-independent'; F1 is a threshold-dependent metric, and the design does not specify which threshold is used, making results non-reproducible.
